faqs.q: Is rejection sensitive dysphoria a real diagnosis?
faqs.a: It isn't a formal diagnosis in the DSM-5-TR. It's a described pattern, most often discussed in connection with ADHD, that names an intense reaction to real or perceived rejection, criticism, or failure. Calling it a pattern doesn't make it less painful or less real.

faqs.q: Why is rejection sensitive dysphoria linked to ADHD?
faqs.a: Many people with ADHD describe strong, fast reactions to perceived rejection, which is why the pattern is most often discussed alongside it. The connection is observed clinically rather than settled as a formal feature, and a clinician looks at the whole picture rather than treating the term as a diagnosis on its own.

faqs.q: Can rejection sensitive dysphoria be helped?
faqs.a: Yes. Strategies that help with emotional regulation, ADHD, and self-criticism tend to help here too. If the reactions are interfering with work, relationships, or daily life, an evaluation can clarify what's driving them and what kind of support fits.
